title: rubus × rupestris
intro: Rubus × rupestris, also known as the wild raspberry or alpine raspberry, is a hybrid species of raspberry that is native to mountainous regions. The plant contains anthocyanins and other flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Rubus × rupestris has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and cardiovascular disease, due to its health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and promoting overall well-being. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
